beep.summary: Plays a waveform sound. The optional <sound type> (0-5) selects which system sound; the default is the Windows Default Beep.

bringupbox.summary: Brings the Tera Term window to the foreground (activates and shows it).

checksum8.summary: Calculates the 8-bit checksum of <string> and stores the result as a numeric value in <intvar>.

checksum8file.summary: Calculates the 8-bit checksum of the file <filename> and stores it in <intvar>. If the file cannot be opened, the system variable result is set to -1.

checksum16.summary: Calculates the 16-bit checksum of <string> and stores the result as a numeric value in <intvar>.

checksum16file.summary: Calculates the 16-bit checksum of the file <filename> and stores it in <intvar>. If the file cannot be opened, the system variable result is set to -1.

checksum32.summary: Calculates the 32-bit checksum of <string> and stores the result as a numeric value in <intvar>.

checksum32file.summary: Calculates the 32-bit checksum of the file <filename> and stores it in <intvar>. If the file cannot be opened, the system variable result is set to -1.

clipb2var.summary: Copies clipboard text into <strvar>, optionally starting at a chunk given by <offset>. Sets result: 0 failure, 1 success, 2 truncated, 3 memory error.

closesbox.summary: Closes the status dialog box that was opened by the statusbox command.

crc16.summary: Calculates the CRC-16 of <string> (polynomial x16+x12+x5+x0) and stores the result as a numeric value in <intvar>.

crc16file.summary: Calculates the CRC-16 of the file <filename> and stores it in <intvar>. If the file cannot be opened, the system variable result is set to -1.

crc32.summary: Calculates the CRC-32 of <string> (Ethernet FCS polynomial) and stores the result as a numeric value in <intvar>.

crc32file.summary: Calculates the CRC-32 of the file <filename> and stores it in <intvar>. If the file cannot be opened, the system variable result is set to -1.

dirnamebox.summary: Opens a dialog to select a directory. The chosen directory name is stored in the system variable inputstr.

exec.summary: Runs an application specified by <command line>, optionally controlling the window show state, whether to wait for it, and the working directory.

filenamebox.summary: Opens a file selection dialog. The chosen filename is stored in the system variable inputstr, and result is set nonzero on selection or zero if canceled.

getdate.summary: Stores the current date in <strvar>, default format YYYY-MM-DD (%Y-%m-%d). <format> uses strftime syntax; an optional <timezone> overrides local time.

getenv.summary: Retrieves the value of the environment variable named <envname> and stores it in the string variable <strvar>.

getipv4addr.summary: Retrieves the local IPv4 address(es) into <string array> and the count into <intvar>. Sets result: 1 success, 0 array overflow, -1 error.

getipv6addr.summary: Retrieves the local IPv6 address(es) into <string array> and the count into <intvar>. Sets result: 1 success, 0 array too small, -1 failure.

getspecialfolder.summary: Stores the path of the Windows special folder identified by <foldertype> into the string variable <strvar>.

gettime.summary: Stores the current time in <strvar>, default format HH:MM:SS (%H:%M:%S). <format> uses strftime syntax; an optional <timezone> overrides local time.

getttdir.summary: Stores the ttpmacro.exe directory into the string variable <strvar>.

getver.summary: Stores the Tera Term version string in <strvar>. If <version> is given, it is compared and result is set: -2 invalid, -1 older, 0 equal, 1 newer.

ifdefined.summary: Sets the system variable result to the type of <var>: 0 undefined, 1 integer, 3 string, 4 label, 5 integer array, 6 string array.

inputbox.summary: Displays a dialog prompting the user to enter a string. The entered text is stored in the system variable inputstr; <default> sets initial text.

intdim.summary: Declares an integer array <array> with <size> entries (1 to 65536, 0-origin index). Default element value is 0.

listbox.summary: Displays a list box of items from <string array>. The selected index (0 to N-1) is stored in result, or -1 if canceled; <selected> pre-selects an item.

messagebox.summary: Displays a dialog box showing <message> with the given <title>. A nonzero <special> enables escape-sequence interpretation (obsolete; use strspecial).

random.summary: Generates a random integer in the range 0..<max number> and assigns it to <integer variable>. <max number> must be greater than 0.

rotateleft.summary: Circularly shifts <intval> left by <count> bits and stores the result in the integer variable <intvar>.

rotateright.summary: Circularly shifts <intval> right by <count> bits and stores the result in the integer variable <intvar>.

setdate.summary: Sets the system date to <date>, which must be in the format YYYY-MM-DD.

setdlgpos.summary: Sets the display position (in pixels) of dialog boxes such as inputbox, listbox, messagebox, statusbox, and yesnobox. With no arguments, dialogs are centered on the primary display.

setenv.summary: Sets the environment variable <envname> to <strval> within the macro environment only (not the system), overwriting it if it already exists.

setexitcode.summary: Sets the macro process exit code, which can be tested afterward via the DOS 'if errorlevel n' command.

settime.summary: Sets the system time to <time>, which must be in the format HH:MM:SS.

show.summary: Controls the macro window: minimizes it if <show flag> is 0, restores and brings it to top if greater than 0, and hides it if less than 0.

statusbox.summary: Displays a status dialog box (or updates its <message> and <title> if already shown). Position it with setdlgpos and close it with closesbox; it cannot be dismissed by the user.

strdim.summary: Declares a string array <array> with <size> entries (1 to 65536, 0-origin index). Default element value is an empty string.

uptime.summary: Stores into <intvar> the number of milliseconds elapsed since Windows started. The value wraps to zero after about 49.7 days of continuous running.

var2clipb.summary: Copies <string> to the clipboard. Sets the system variable result to 0 if the clipboard could not be opened, or 1 on success.

yesnobox.summary: Displays a Yes/No dialog box with <message> and <title>. The system variable result is set to 1 if Yes is clicked, or 0 if No.
